---
title: "Getting Help: The Quantitative Analytics and Statistics Guild"
---

The Quantitative Analytics and Statistics Guild (the Guild) provides specialist statistical and analytical support across CQC. This page tells you how to access that support.

## What the Guild Does

The Guild supports analysts working on statistical and quantitative questions in CQC's regulatory and improvement work. It is the professional home for statistical expertise within the organisation and offers:

- Peer review and sense-checking of analytical approaches
- Specialist advice on methods beyond routine profiling (multiple imputation, multilevel models, causal inference, etc.)
- Guidance on new indicator development and fitness-for-purpose assessments
- Support for QA decisions where statistical judgment is required
- A space to raise emerging issues and share learning across teams

The Guild does not make operational decisions — that remains with the commissioning team. It provides analytical advice to inform those decisions.

## When to Come to the Guild

Come to the Guild when you encounter any of the following:

| Situation | Guild route |
|-----------|------------|
| Assumption violations you're unsure how to handle | Quant Clinic drop-in |
| Method selection beyond routine profiling (regression, multilevel, imputation) | Quant Clinic or direct contact |
| New indicator development requiring statistical sign-off | Direct contact with Practice Lead |
| Stakeholder challenge to methodology | Quant Clinic or escalation |
| Working z-scores, funnel plots, advanced SPC | Quant Clinic drop-in |
| Multiple testing corrections for high-stakes analysis | Quant Clinic drop-in |
| MNAR or substantial missing data (>20%) | Quant Clinic or escalation |

Every methods chapter in Part II includes a "When to Seek Support from the Guild" section with specific triggers for that method.

## Meeting Cadence

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## Note for Document Reviewers

The dates and links in this section should be verified against current Guild scheduling before publication. Contact the Practice Lead to confirm the current cadence and any Teams channel details.
:::

### Quant Clinic — Drop-in Sessions

**Format**: Open drop-in — no agenda, no booking required. Bring your question.

**Frequency**: Fortnightly (every two weeks)

**Duration**: 1 hour

**Who attends**: Any analyst with a statistical or methodological question. Guild members rotate as hosts.

**What to bring**: A brief description of your question, the data you're working with, and what you've tried so far. You don't need slides.

### Monthly Guild Meeting

**Format**: Structured agenda — updates, knowledge-sharing, emerging issues

**Frequency**: Monthly

**Duration**: 1 hour

**Who attends**: Guild members and invited guests. Open to observers — contact the Practice Lead if you'd like to attend a session.

**Agenda items typically include**: Method spotlight, peer review of a live analysis, updates from external bodies (GSS, ONS, AQuA), emerging issues from operational teams.

## How to Contact the Guild

**Teams channel**: The Guild has a dedicated Microsoft Teams channel for asynchronous questions. Post your question there and a Guild member will respond, typically within two working days.

**Practice Lead**: For questions requiring direct escalation, methodological sign-off, or access to validated code (e.g., Working z-score scripts), contact the Practice Lead directly.

**Peer review requests**: If your analysis will inform a high-stakes decision (enforcement, public reporting), consider requesting a formal peer review. Contact the Practice Lead to arrange this.

## Related Resources

- **How to Use This Guidance** → @sec-getting-started for an overview of the guidance structure and how the Guild fits in
- **Competency Framework Alignment** → @sec-competency-alignment for how Guild support maps to professional development
- **QA Framework** → @sec-qa-principles for the five questions that structure analytical quality assurance
